DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY FROM DEFENDANTS, OR ALTERNATIVELY, TO IMPOSE ISSUE SANCTIONS, EVIDENCE SANCTIONS, AND/OR OTHER TERMINATING SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS, AND FOR FURTHER MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AS IT SEEKS FURTHER MONETARY SANCTIONS. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND THAT ISSUE, EVIDENTIARY, OR TERMINATING SANCTIONS ARE WARRANTED AT THIS TIME. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE LATE COMPLIANCE WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,250, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery From Defts, Or Alternatively, To Impose Issue Sanctions, Evidence Sanctions And/Or Other Terminating Sanctionsa Gainst Defts, And For Further Monetary Sanctions;
Off Calendar Sep-01-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
BARRY K. TAGAWA, ESQ., (415)951-8600, FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, JOHN STANLEY
DAVID L. WINNETT, ESQ. OF HINSHAW & CULBERTSON LLP, (415)362-6000, FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS ADOPTED. Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Discovery From Defendants, or alternatively, To Impose Issue Sanctions, Evidence Sanctions, and/or Other Terminating Sanctions Against Defendants, And For Further Monetary Sanctions is granted as it seeks further monetary sanctions. The court does not find that issue, evidentiary, or terminating sanctions are warranted at this time. The court finds that the late compliance was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $2,250, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (612/EAH) COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order To Vacate Prior Discovery Orders
Off Calendar Mar-22-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Nicholas T. Hua, appeared for Defendant/Moving party Contractors Source Insurance
Barry K. Tagawa, appeared for Plaintiff/Opposing party John Stanley
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Contractors Source Insurance Services, Inc.,'s Motion For An Order To Vacate Prior Discovery Orders is granted as it seeks to set aside the order deeming Requests For Admission admitted. There has been a determination that there was excusable neglect in Defendant's failure to participate in the litigation. That determination resulted in an order setting aside Defendant's default and the bases of the order deeming the requests admitted. Defendant is ordered to respond to the discovery that was the subject of the previous orders within 10 days of notice of this order.COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Off Calendar Sep-27-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Barry K. Tagawa for plaintiff John Stanley and David Winnett for defendant Contractors Source Ins. Services.

Hearing is held. Default of defendant is ordered entered. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's Motion To Impose Issue Sanctions, Evidence Sanctions and/or Other Terminating Sanctions Against Defendants, and For Further Monetary Sanctions is granted as it seeks terminating sanctions.
